title: Agent Spend Policy
description: The buyer-side payment contract. Declares what an agent is authorized to spend and under what conditions. Operators configure this; agents enforce it at runtime. IMPORTANT: Tools that check spend policy must extract it from the verified X-OpenTrust-Agent-Identity JWT (the spend_policy field), not from the standalone X-OpenTrust-Spend-Policy header. The header is a convenience hint for tools that do a quick pre-flight check; the JWT-embedded copy is the authoritative, signed version. A standalone header not backed by the JWT must be treated as unauthenticated.

max_cost_per_call_usdc.description: Hard cap per individual tool call. Agent must reject any tool whose pricing.amount exceeds this.

max_cost_per_session_usdc.description: Hard cap across all tool calls within one agent session/task.

max_cost_per_day_usdc.description: Hard cap per calendar day across all sessions.

min_trust_status.description: Minimum trust_status a tool must have before the agent will pay for it. Recommended: community_reviewed or higher for any tool with network or file access.

blocked_permissions.description: Permissions the agent is not authorized to pay for. If a tool declares any of these in its permission_manifest, the agent must refuse to pay and must not call the tool.

allowed_networks.description: Which chains the agent is authorized to transact on. If the tool's payment_config.network is not in this list, the agent must not pay.

allowed_currencies.description: Which currencies the agent is authorized to spend.

require_escrow_above_usdc.description: For any single payment above this amount, the agent must use escrow. If the tool does not support escrow and the payment exceeds this threshold, the agent must refuse.

human_approval_above_usdc.description: For any single payment above this amount, the agent must pause and request human approval before proceeding. Only meaningful for agent_type 'supervised' or 'human_in_the_loop'. If this field is set on an 'autonomous' agent identity token, the agent must treat it as a hard block (refuse the call entirely, do not proceed) rather than a pause — autonomous agents have no human to ask. Implementers should reject a spend policy with human_approval_above_usdc if the enclosing agent identity has agent_type: 'autonomous'.

blocked_categories.description: Tool categories (from the taxonomy enum) the agent is not authorized to pay for.

allowed_registries.description: If set, only tools whose passports are registered at one of these registries may be paid. Empty = any trusted registry is allowed.

max_orchestration_depth.description: Maximum sub-agent spawning depth this policy allows. An agent at depth N may not spawn sub-agents if N >= max_orchestration_depth. Prevents runaway agent chains. 0 = this agent may not spawn any sub-agents.

sub_agent_policy.description: How spend policy propagates to sub-agents spawned by this agent. inherit: sub-agents get the same policy unchanged. restrict: sub-agents get this policy with max_cost_per_call and max_cost_per_session divided by depth+1, enforcing progressively tighter budgets. deny: this agent may not spawn sub-agents that incur costs.

allow_spawning_agents.description: Whether this agent is permitted to call tools whose source_formats includes 'agent'. Set to false to prevent the agent from invoking other agents entirely.

dynamic_budget_allocation.description: EXPERIMENTAL — disabled by default. Allows per-sub-agent budget caps (absolute and percentage-based) that can be adjusted at runtime by a human operator or a trusted AI agent. Enable only if your orchestration system can reliably enforce real-time allocation updates. This feature is subject to breaking changes before stabilization. IMPORTANT — JWT size: when embedding a spend policy in an agent identity JWT, the allocations array grows the token by ~100 bytes per entry. HTTP headers are typically limited to 8KB. For policies with more than ~40 named allocations, implementations should strip dynamic_budget_allocation from the JWT and instead use a policy_url field pointing to a signed endpoint that serves the full policy. See docs/agent-key-management.md.

enabled.description: Must be explicitly set to true to activate dynamic allocation. When false, the rest of this object is ignored and standard sub_agent_policy rules apply.

allocations.description: Per-sub-agent budget entries. Matched by agent_id or agent_slug. If a sub-agent is not listed, it falls back to the standard restrict/inherit/deny rules.

match.description: Identifies which sub-agent(s) this allocation applies to. At least one of agent_id or agent_slug must be provided.

agent_slug.description: Slug-only match — applies to any agent with this slug regardless of registry or org.

max_usdc.description: Absolute USDC hard cap for this sub-agent across its lifetime in this session. The sub-agent cannot spend more than this regardless of the parent's remaining budget.

budget_percent.description: Percentage of the parent agent's REMAINING budget at the moment this sub-agent is spawned. Evaluated at spawn time using an atomic read-then-reserve of the parent's remaining budget — the orchestrator must atomically reserve this allocation before spawning the sub-agent to prevent concurrent spawning races. Example: parent has $10 remaining, budget_percent=40 → sub-agent is allocated $4.00 and the parent's available-to-allocate balance drops to $6.00 immediately, regardless of whether other sub-agents are spawning concurrently. When both max_usdc and budget_percent are set, the lower value wins. If the parent's remaining budget is $0, the sub-agent receives $0 and must not be spawned.

priority.description: Tiebreak order when multiple allocation entries match the same sub-agent. Lower number = higher priority. Defaults to 100 if omitted.

allocation_controller.description: Who or what is authorized to push real-time allocation updates via budget_adjustment_endpoint. Without a controller, allocations are static (set once at policy issue time).

type.description: human_operator: only authenticated human operators may adjust. ai_agent: a named trusted agent may adjust. both: either may adjust.

agent_id.description: agent_id of the AI agent authorized to push adjustments. Required when type is 'ai_agent' or 'both'.

operator_identity.description: GitHub username, org slug, or domain of the human operator authorized to adjust. Required when type is 'human_operator' or 'both'.

budget_adjustment_endpoint.description: HTTPS endpoint the orchestrator exposes so the allocation_controller can push updated per-sub-agent caps mid-session. Accepts a PATCH body matching the allocations array schema. Authentication is REQUIRED — the endpoint must verify the controller's identity before applying changes. For allocation_controller.type 'human_operator': use OAuth 2.0 Bearer token or equivalent. For allocation_controller.type 'ai_agent': the requesting agent must include a valid X-OpenTrust-Agent-Identity JWT whose agent_id matches allocation_controller.agent_id, and the orchestrator must verify the JWT signature. For type 'both': accept either authentication method. The endpoint must respond with 200 and the updated allocations on success, or 403 if authentication fails. If absent, allocations are read-only after policy issue.

unallocated_fallback.description: What to do for sub-agents not matched by any allocations entry. 'restrict' applies the standard depth-based division from sub_agent_policy=restrict. 'deny' blocks them from spending entirely.

audit_log_endpoint.description: Optional endpoint that receives a structured event every time a sub-agent's budget is consumed or an allocation adjustment is applied. Useful for human review of autonomous allocation decisions.

policy_version.description: Semantic version of this spend policy document. Operators bump this when policy changes.
